definition: To perform a task or present oneself exceptionally well; often paired as 'ate and left no crumbs.'
exampleSentence: She absolutely ate that presentation — professor was speechless.
origin: African American Vernacular English (AAVE), popularized via drag culture and social media.

definition: Mainstream, unoriginal, or uncool; a mild insult toward someone who follows trends without originality.
exampleSentence: Let's get out of here. This party is basic.
origin: Popularized in early 2010s internet culture and hip-hop.

definition: Authentically and unapologetically oneself, especially when holding a controversial or unpopular opinion.
exampleSentence: You brought your own snacks to the cinema? Based behavior.
origin: Originated from rapper Lil B's philosophy and spread through internet forums.

definition: Extremely good or delicious; primarily used in the context of food.
exampleSentence: These tacos are bussin', I need another plate!
origin: African American Vernacular English (AAVE), popularized by food-related TikTok content.

definition: A lie or exaggeration. 'Capping' means lying.
exampleSentence: He said he won the lottery, but I think he's capping.
origin: African American Vernacular English (AAVE). 'No cap' is the negation.

definition: An intensifier meaning 'for real' or 'honestly'; used to assert that the speaker is telling the truth.
exampleSentence: No cap, that was the best movie I've ever seen.
origin: African American Vernacular English (AAVE), popularized via hip-hop and social media.

definition: Outdated, uncool, or trying too hard to be trendy; specifically targets aesthetics from the early 2010s.
exampleSentence: I can't believe she's still wearing skinny jeans, that's so cheugy.
origin: Coined in 2013, popularized on TikTok in 2021.

definition: High quality, excellent, or impressive; originally rooted in cannabis culture, now applied broadly.
exampleSentence: The party last night was dank!
origin: Originally English slang for 'damp,' repurposed in cannabis culture and internet meme culture.

definition: Over-the-top, dramatic, or excessive behavior that is often unnecessary.
exampleSentence: Her outfit for the party was extra with all the sequins and feathers.
origin: African American Vernacular English (AAVE).

definition: Amazing, impressive, or exceptionally good; synonymous with 'lit.'
exampleSentence: That new song is fire.
origin: Hip-hop slang that crossed into mainstream internet use.

definition: Exciting, fun, or amazing; describes a high-energy situation or object.
exampleSentence: This party is lit! The music is great and everyone is having a good time.
origin: Hip-hop slang, mainstream since mid-2010s.

definition: Mediocre, average, or underwhelming; used to describe something that fails to meet expectations.
exampleSentence: That movie was hyped up but ended up being very mid.
origin: Internet slang, popularized on Twitch and gaming communities.

definition: Bitter, annoyed, or upset, typically over a minor or trivial matter.
exampleSentence: I lost the game to my friend. That's why I'm just a little salty about it.
origin: Popularized in gaming culture and social media in the 2010s.

definition: Looking flawless, stylish, or physically fit; frequently used to compliment an outfit or appearance.
exampleSentence: Her outfit for the party was snatched. Everyone was talking about it.
origin: LGBTQ+ ballroom culture, popularized via drag culture and TikTok.

definition: Short for 'suspicious'; refers to someone or something that appears sketchy or untrustworthy.
exampleSentence: Why is he acting so sus? What are you hiding?
origin: Popularized by the game Among Us (2020), but originally 90s slang.

definition: Cool, acceptable, or logically sound; used to validate an opinion or feeling.
exampleSentence: That opinion is valid.
origin: Widespread internet slang derived from formal English validation.

definition: Lively, bold, or flamboyant; can also refer to something dramatic or cheeky.
exampleSentence: That outfit is zesty.
origin: Internet slang, coded language in LGBTQ+ online spaces.

definition: An absurdist adjective or filler word that can mean good, bad, cool, or evil depending on context.
exampleSentence: That's so skibidi — I don't even know what to say.
origin: Alexey Gerasimov's 'Skibidi Toilet' YouTube series — heads in toilets singing a repetitive song.

definition: A descriptor for anything strange, weird, or cringe-worthy. References the 'Only in Ohio' meme.
exampleSentence: That is so Ohio — I cannot explain what just happened.
origin: 'Only in Ohio' meme depicting the US state as a site of supernatural or chaotic events.

definition: The act of stealing or taking a portion of a friend's food.
exampleSentence: Don't leave your fries near him — he'll apply the Fanum Tax.
origin: Streamer Kai Cenat and his friend Fanum, who frequently 'taxed' Kai's food on stream.

definition: Mental decline from consuming excessive low-quality digital content; used as a self-aware label.
standardEnglish: Excessive social media consumption / cultural decline
exampleSentence: I've been watching Skibidi Toilet for 3 hours. The brainrot is real.
origin: Concept of mental decline repurposed as an ironic, self-aware internet culture term.

definition: Describes something or someone ridiculous, silly, or funny in an absurd way.
exampleSentence: He showed up in a goofy ahh outfit to the formal event.
origin: Phonetic variation of 'Goofy ass,' spread through meme culture.

definition: To lose control, have a meltdown, or act recklessly due to frustration.
exampleSentence: He crashed out when they canceled his favorite show.
origin: Internet vernacular describing an intense emotional outburst.

definition: An orthodontic tongue-posture technique popularized for jawline definition; also a 'no talking' gesture.
standardEnglish: Jaw-defining tongue posture exercise; signaling silence
exampleSentence: He's been mewing for six months — can't tell if it's working.
origin: Popularized by Dr. Mike Mew's orthodontic technique, became a meme signifying working on appearance.

definition: The most dominant, powerful, or assertive person in a group; sometimes used mockingly.
exampleSentence: He thinks he's the alpha of the friend group, but it's just cringe.
origin: Borrowed from evolutionary biology, popularized by internet self-improvement communities.

definition: A 'lone wolf' leader who is successful and independent, existing outside the Alpha/Beta hierarchy.
exampleSentence: He has major sigma energy — he doesn't care what anyone thinks.
origin: Evolved from 'sigma male' meme, popularized through internet self-improvement content.

definition: Someone with a high degree of 'rizz' (charisma); a top-tier charmer.
exampleSentence: He's the ultimate rizzler; he can talk to anyone.
origin: Derived from 'rizz,' popularized by streamer Kai Cenat.

definition: 'Non-Player Character' — someone who lacks originality and seems to follow a pre-set script.
standardEnglish: Someone who is boring or unthinking
exampleSentence: I felt like an NPC in that meeting, just nodding along.
origin: Gaming term for computer-controlled characters, adopted as social commentary.

definition: Short for 'Uncle'; refers to an older person or someone out of touch with modern trends.
exampleSentence: I couldn't figure out how to repost on TikTok — I feel so unc.
origin: Abbreviation of 'uncle,' used to describe generational disconnection.

definition: A stereotypically hyper-masculine, confident male; often used humorously.
exampleSentence: He's such a Chad with that workout routine.
origin: Originated in internet incel culture, widely adopted ironically.

definition: An obsessive or highly dedicated fan; portmanteau of 'stalker' and 'fan.'
exampleSentence: I stan Beyoncé — she's the queen of everything.
origin: From Eminem's 2000 song 'Stan,' depicting an obsessive fan.

definition: A term of endearment for a loved one or best friend.
exampleSentence: That's my pookie — we go everywhere together.
origin: General endearment term, popularized through social media affection.

definition: Short for 'opposition'; an enemy, rival, or someone the speaker dislikes.
exampleSentence: He hangs out with the opps now? Wild.
origin: Hip-hop slang that crossed into mainstream internet use.

definition: A romantic or sexual relationship that lacks clear labels or commitment; 'more than friends, less than a relationship.'
exampleSentence: We don't see other people but we aren't official… it's a situationship.
origin: Emerged from Gen Z's reluctance to commit to relationship labels, popularized on social media.

definition: A sudden, irreversible feeling of disgust toward a romantic interest triggered by a minor habit or trait.
exampleSentence: He chewed with his mouth open and I got the ick immediately.
origin: Popularized on TikTok and dating discourse, particularly from creator Olivia Neill.

definition: Suddenly and completely ending communication with someone without explanation.
exampleSentence: He ghosted me after our first date — so rude!
origin: Dating culture term from the 2010s, widely adopted in social media discourse.

definition: Subtly introducing a new partner on social media without showing their face.
standardEnglish: Subtly hinting at a new relationship on social media
exampleSentence: She posted his shoes in her story — classic soft launch.
origin: Borrowed from marketing terminology and applied to relationship reveals on social media.

definition: The definitive, public reveal of a relationship through clear social media posts.
standardEnglish: Officially announcing a relationship on social media
exampleSentence: Two weeks after the soft launch, she finally hard launched him on IG.
origin: Counterpart to 'soft launch,' borrowed from marketing terminology.

definition: Truncated from 'charisma'; refers to one's ability to charm or flirt successfully.
exampleSentence: He's got mad rizz — everyone loves him.
origin: Popularized by Twitch streamer and YouTuber Kai Cenat around 2021.

definition: A secret romantic or casual relationship kept hidden from others.
exampleSentence: They've been a sneaky link for months now.
origin: Popularized through hip-hop and social media in early 2020s.

definition: Someone who displays excessive affection or submissiveness to a crush, often without reciprocation.
exampleSentence: Stop simping for her — she's not even interested.
origin: Old English slang repopularized through Twitch streaming culture around 2019.

definition: Short for 'Win.' Used to rate an action, person, or event as a success.
exampleSentence: He got promoted and got a raise in the same week. Massive W.
origin: Gaming culture shorthand, adopted widely in social media commentary.

definition: Short for 'Loss.' Used to rate an action, person, or event as a failure.
exampleSentence: He tripped in front of everyone. That's a big L.
origin: Gaming culture shorthand, adopted widely in social media commentary.

definition: Short for 'Good Game.' Used at the end of a match; can also be used sarcastically to mean 'it's over.'
exampleSentence: They got completely demolished in the tournament. GG.
origin: Competitive gaming culture, now used broadly as social commentary.

definition: Occurs when a reply to a post gets more likes than the original post, indicating widespread disagreement.
standardEnglish: Publicly proven wrong by community response
exampleSentence: He made a terrible take and got absolutely ratioed.
origin: Twitter/X culture term for when a reply surpasses the original post in engagement.

definition: Proof or evidence of a claim, typically in the form of screenshots or text messages.
exampleSentence: She said she never said that but I have the receipts.
origin: Popularized through celebrity drama and social media call-out culture.

definition: Fear Of Missing Out. The anxiety that others are having rewarding experiences from which one is absent.
exampleSentence: I couldn't make it to the concert and the FOMO is real.
origin: Coined by Patrick J. McGinnis in 2004, popularized through social media.

definition: If You Know You Know. Indicates an inside joke understood only by a specific group.
standardEnglish: Inside joke / You had to be there
exampleSentence: The conference was absolutely wild. IYKYK.
origin: Internet acronym that became widely used across social media platforms.

definition: Point Of View. Used to describe a scene from a specific perspective; common in TikTok skits.
standardEnglish: Point of view / Imagine you are...
exampleSentence: POV: you're late to class and the teacher just locked the door.
origin: Film/literature term adopted by TikTok creators to frame skits.

definition: Seriously; used to emphasize that one is telling the absolute truth.
exampleSentence: Deadass, that was the scariest thing I've ever seen.
origin: New York City slang, popularized nationally through internet culture.

definition: Indicates that the speaker has understood the point and no further explanation is needed.
exampleSentence: Need a ride to the airport? Say less, I'll be there at 6.
origin: African American Vernacular English (AAVE), popularized via hip-hop.

definition: Social media influence, fame, or power. 'Clout chasing' means doing things for attention.
exampleSentence: He only hangs out with celebrities for the clout.
origin: Hip-hop slang for influence, adopted widely by social media culture.

definition: An aesthetic centered on simple, rural living, traditional crafts, and harmony with nature.
exampleSentence: Her whole Instagram is pure cottagecore — bread baking and wildflowers.
origin: Niche internet aesthetic community that emerged around 2018–2020.

definition: A hyper-feminine aesthetic dominated by vibrant pinks and inspired by the Barbie franchise.
exampleSentence: She showed up to the party in full Barbiecore — head-to-toe pink.
origin: Emerged in 2023 alongside the Barbie film, popularized by fashion influencers.

definition: Social media-driven alternative aesthetic featuring heavy eyeliner, dyed hair, and 90s/emo influences.
exampleSentence: He's got full e-boy vibes with the striped long-sleeve under the graphic tee.
origin: Emerged on TikTok around 2019, rooted in e-culture and emo subculture.

definition: Embracing a gentle, relaxed, aesthetically pleasing lifestyle; often a rejection of hustle culture.
exampleSentence: She's fully embraced the soft girl era — no drama, just vibes.
origin: TikTok aesthetic trend emphasizing pastel colors, femininity, and emotional openness.

definition: A shift in wealth definition away from money toward experiences, health, and curiosity.
standardEnglish: Wealthy in experiences and fulfillment, not money
exampleSentence: I'm not making bank but I'm genuinely rich in life right now.
origin: Emerging cultural counter-narrative to material wealth definitions, 2025–2026.

definition: Describing someone who is mentally absent, clueless, or checked out — referencing the HTTP 'Not Found' error.
exampleSentence: He had no idea what we were talking about. Fully 404 coded.
origin: Tech-savvy internet slang borrowing from HTTP error codes, emerging 2025–2026.

definition: Framing unexpected detours or minor tasks as adventures in the 'game' of real life.
standardEnglish: Unexpected but interesting detour or task
exampleSentence: Getting lost led us to the best restaurant — total side quest moment.
origin: Gaming metaphor applied to real life, popularized through life-as-game internet framing.

definition: A defining life moment — often chaotic or difficult — viewed as essential to personal development.
exampleSentence: Getting rejected from your first choice school is such a canon event.
origin: Narrative/storytelling concept applied to personal life, popularized by TikTok in 2023–2024.

definition: Performatively curating one's vibe or image to gain social 'aura points' rather than being authentic.
exampleSentence: He doesn't actually meditate — he's just aura farming for Instagram.
origin: Derived from gaming 'farming' mechanics, applied to social media performance, 2025–2026.

definition: An unapologetic, messy, and carefree aesthetic trend defined by lime-green visuals and fearlessness.
exampleSentence: She's fully in her brat summer era — not apologizing for anything.
origin: Coined by Charli XCX in 2024 with her album 'brat,' defining a cultural moment.
